blocks.text: The same payload renders on **eight surfaces**: web, Google Apps Script web apps, Meet stage, Chat, side panel, email, PDF, and MCP Apps. Each surface implements the atoms it supports; the payload doesn't change. That's the portability dividend of keeping UI as data.

items.label: Serialize
items.text: JSON → gzip (level 9, wbits 31) → URL-safe base64, padding stripped

items.label: Ship
items.text: The blob rides in the URL itself: .../exec?p=<blob> — the link IS the app, nothing stored anywhere

items.label: Decode
items.text: The renderer reverses the encoding and dispatches blocks as usual

blocks.text: **Ceilings, per page:** ~2,000 characters is copy-safe (terminals and chat clients truncate long lines); ~8,000 is the hard decode limit on Apps Script. A truncated `?p=` is a truncated gzip stream — it fails loudly, not partially.

items.label: Scale path A — paginate
items.text: module_map modules carrying a page: array auto-encode into self-contained ?p= sub-pages at render time — a real Apps Script Utilities.gzip() call, GAS-server-side. Simple, no publishing step.

items.label: Scale path A′ — pre-publish
items.text: Browsers can't run that same encode synchronously (no sync gzip in JS), so on MCP Apps, module_map cards carry a url: to an already-minted permanent link instead — same atom, same card, a different mechanism chosen for the environment it's rendering in.

items.label: Scale path B — graduate
items.text: emit_deployment bakes the payload into a standing Apps Script app — no URL, no size limit, server-side named pages. Best for real, reusable content.

blocks.title: You are inside the example — the other path
blocks.text: This module is its own permanently-published short link (minted via publish_url), not a runtime ?p= encode — that's Scale path A′ above. The module_map atom looks identical either way; only the mechanism behind url: vs page: changes with where it's rendered.

blocks.question: A payload encodes to 12,000 characters. What's the right move?
options: Ship it — URLs have no practical limit
Split into module_map pages, or graduate it into a standing app
blocks.explanation: The ~8k ceiling is per page and hard (GAS can't decode past it) — and truncating gzip destroys the stream. Pagination and graduation are the two designed scale paths.

points: One payload, eight surfaces — the payload never changes
?p= links are apps with zero storage: the URL carries the gzipped payload
~2k copy-safe, ~8k hard — per page, never truncate
module_map adapts per environment: page: (runtime ?p=) on GAS, url: (pre-published) on MCP Apps
Paginate (module_map) to stay shareable; graduate (emit_deployment) for no limits
